lede: A look at September 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jennifer Tour Chayes and Ernesto Teodoro Moneta, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Don't ask, don't tell
events.desc: The United States military ends its "don't ask, don't tell" policy, allowing gay men and women to serve openly for the first time.

events.title: Islamabad Marriott Hotel bombing
events.desc: A dump truck full of explosives detonates in front of the Marriott hotel in Islamabad, Pakistan, killing 54 people and injuring 266 others.

events.title: Vietnam
events.desc: Vietnam is admitted to the United Nations.

events.title: Hurricane Irene–Olivia
events.desc: Having weakened after making landfall in Nicaragua the previous day, Hurricane Irene regains enough strength to be renamed Hurricane Olivia, making it the first known hurricane to cross from the Atlantic Ocean into the Pacific.

events.title: Irish War of Independence
events.desc: Irish War of Independence: British police known as "Black and Tans" burn the town of Balbriggan and kill two local men in revenge for an IRA assassination.

events.title: Battle of Valmy
events.desc: French troops stop an allied invasion of France at the Battle of Valmy.

events.title: Peace of Ryswick
events.desc: The Treaty of Ryswick is signed by France, England, Spain, the Holy Roman Empire and the Dutch Republic, ending the Nine Years' War.

events.title: Ferdinand Magellan
events.desc: Ferdinand Magellan sets sail from Sanlúcar de Barrameda with about 270 men on his expedition which ultimately culminates in the first circumnavigation of the globe.

events.title: 1498 Meiō earthquake
events.desc: The Nankai tsunami washes away the building housing the Great Buddha at Kōtoku-in; it has been located outside ever since.

events.title: Prussian uprisings
events.desc: The Great Prussian Uprising among the old Prussians begins against the Teutonic Knights.

births.name: Ernesto Teodoro Moneta
births.line: Italian soldier and journalist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Victor Ponta
births.line: Romanian jurist and politician, 63rd Prime Minister of Romania

deaths.text: Simon Wiesenthal, Austrian human rights activist, Holocaust survivor
José Gaspar Rodríguez de Francia, Paraguayan lawyer and politician, Consul of Paraguay
Conrad II of Salzwedel, German nobleman and bishop

observances.text: Christian feast day: Jean-Charles Cornay (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
Christian feast day: John Coleridge Patteson (commemoration, Anglicanism)
Christian feast day: José Maria de Yermo y Parres
